title: Myrcia millspaughii
taxonomy: Myrcia millspaughii is classified in the family Melastomataceae. This family is a part of the order Myrtales and the clade Rosids. The clade Rosids is further classified within the larger clade of eudicots, which is a subset of angiosperms. Angiosperms are a group of flowering plants that also include gymnosperms and other plant groups. Overall, Myrcia millspaughii's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Myrcia millspaughii, also known as "Cuphea" or "Mexican Heather", is a small shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Myrcia millspaughii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, and it is also used in some herbal preparations to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
